title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
Scope determines where variables are accessible in your code.

• Global scope — declared outside any function/block; accessible everywhere.
• Function scope — variables declared with var inside a function; only visible inside that function.
• Block scope — variables declared with let/const inside {} (if, for, etc.); only visible inside that block.
• Module scope — each ES6 module file has its own scope by default.

The scope chain: when JS looks for a variable, it starts in the current scope and works outward to the global scope.
